theme: engineered mud floods rebuild a sinking marsh
title: Delta Foreman: Scheduling Mud Floods to Out-Build a Sinking Marsh
innovation_hook: Deposits are not just score, they are terrain that steers future flow -- the release schedule is a program controlling a self-modifying router.
strategies: trivial baseline: dump the whole budget in the final release; it channelises straight down the incised trap-channel to the deep-water edge and builds almost no land (this is the checker's reference B)
greedy recipe: spread the budget uniformly over all T releases; splitting into pulses beats a single dump, but a flat schedule under-invests early and never programs an efficient trap

strong insight: a crescendo (geometric ramp-up), searched and locally refined against the exact simulator -- small early pulses are spent to aggrade a near-shore platform that flattens the local slope and steers later flow sideways, so the big late releases land on engineered terrain and are retained in full despite subsidence
trap: releasing everything early (or naively late to dodge subsidence) both fail -- early mass subsides away, late mass channelises out to sea; only shaping-then-filling wins
